+++
title = "Work Experiences : Data Analyst"
date = "2022-04-05"
+++

First of all, I want to mention that this company is a startup, so I anticipated that I would encounter a variety of tasks when joining. In one position, I would likely have to handle different types of work, so I am not hesitant to submit a job application.

And as expected, that's exactly what happened.

<!--more-->

# About the company
The company I work for is a e-commerce startup. Our role is to act as a middleman to enable Thai businesses to export their products and expand into neighboring countries more easily. We have incorporated various technologies to facilitate convenience in every step of the process.

# My Roles
In my position as a **Data Analyst**, I gather data from various sources within the organization and store it in a data warehouse, using the ELT (Extract-Load-Transform) process, which utilizes Google Big Query on the Google Cloud Platform.

The various sources of data is a significant pain point for the team, as analyzing data from different locations takes up a lot of time each day. Having a single place to gather all the data would be more efficient.

![data scouces](data:image/png;base64,)

The picture shows various sources of data, including :

- **Google Sheet** : contains information on various operational activities, as well as operating costs in different areas, such as advertising costs, which are separated into multiple sheets, making it difficult to analyze.

- **ERP** : Sales transaction data that occurs in the Enterprise Resource Planning system (ERP) is also stored in the data warehouse to be analyzed together with cost data from Google Sheet.

- **CRM** : Finally, the data that cannot be missed in the analysis is the customer behavior data.

Once the data is in the data warehouse, the most difficult task is data cleaning. As mentioned above, almost all of them was manual by human, which cannot escape the problem of human error. But in the end, I was done perfect successfully.

As we all know, images have more impact than table data. Therefore, once the data is ready, the next step is to visualize it for various teams to use, presented in a dashboard to solve the pain points mentioned earlier. The tool used for this is still Google's, namely Google Data Studio, or its new name is Looker Studio.

### Dashboard Examples

![dashboard 1](data:image/png;base64,)

![dashboard 2](data:image/png;base64,)

![dashboard3.png](data:image/png;base64,)


Finally, the dashboard we created was able to solve pain points and we received feedback, as follows :
> The dashboard helped reduce time, coordination, and steps in the work process. This is because the necessary data that everyone should see and use has been collected and analyzed in all dashboards.

> The dashboard came from real data and from the same data source, which reduces the chances of receiving erroneous data, communication errors, or other problems that arise from human error.

> All departments see the same overall picture, both opportunities and problems, which allows them to take action quickly and precisely.


And all of this is my experience working as a Data Analyst.

---
Pakkapon Tontiwich
